channel.title: Inference — AI Newsletter
channel.description: The signal in the noise. Curated AI trends, tools, and research — updated every Monday.

item.title: Trump moves to ban Anthropic from the US government
item.description: Trump is getting traction for a reason. Skim the details before your next sprint (via Ars Technica AI).
item.author: Will Knight, WIRED.com

item.title: [Boost]
item.description: Worth a quick read — Boost tackles a gap most teams are still working around (via Dev.to AI).
item.author: Omar Corredor Pacheco

item.title: CoPaw
item.description: Concise coverage of CoPaw. Read it with your current stack in mind (via GitHub Trending (AI/ML)).
item.author: agentscope-ai

item.title: Show HN: IssueScout – Find open source issues worth contributing to
item.description: Open distribution of IssueScout lowers adoption barriers and pressures proprietary alternatives (via Hacker News).

item.title: Letting Machines Decide What Matters
item.description: Concise coverage of Letting Machines Decide What Matters. Read it with your current stack in mind (via IEEE Spectrum AI).
item.author: Eliza Strickland

item.title: Google AI Introduces STATIC: A Sparse Matrix Framework Delivering 948x Faster Constrained Decoding for LLM Based Generative Retrieval
item.description: STATIC going open lowers the bar for experimentation and custom deployments (via MarkTechPost).
item.author: Asif Razzaq

item.title: OpenAI and Amazon announce strategic partnership
item.description: The OpenAI announcement carries implications for budgets and vendor relationships (via OpenAI Blog).

item.title: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
item.description: OpenAI is getting traction for a reason. Skim the details before your next sprint (via TechCrunch AI).
item.author: Anthony Ha

item.title: A robot arm with puppy dog eyes is just one of Lenovo’s new desktop AI concepts
item.description: Concise coverage of Lenovo. Read it with your current stack in mind (via The Verge AI).
item.author: Andrew Liszewski

item.title: The Data Centers Have Arrived at the Edge of the Arctic Circle
item.description: Worth a quick read — Data Centres Have Arrived tackles a gap most teams are still working around (via Wired AI).

item.title: FireRedTeam Releases FireRed-OCR-2B Utilizing GRPO to Solve Structural Hallucinations in Tables and LaTeX for Software Developers
item.description: Concise coverage of FireRed-OCR-2B Utilizing GRPO. Read it with your current stack in mind (via MarkTechPost).

item.title: Firefox 149 beta develops a split personality
item.description: Firefox is getting traction for a reason. Skim the details before your next sprint (via The Register).

item.title: Iran all but vanishes from the global internet amid US-Israel strikes
item.description: Quick update on Iran — relevant if you're evaluating tools in this space (via The Register).

item.title: Microsoft's Project Silica promises eternal storage. It can't get there from here
item.description: Microsoft's Project Silica is getting traction for a reason. Skim the details before your next sprint (via The Register).
